commit.message:
fix(notifications): use valid type values matching DB check constraint

The notifications table has a check constraint allowing only 'critical',
'warning', 'info' types. The code was using 'process_created',
'allocation_completed', 'process_completed' which caused 400 errors.

commit.message:
Downgrade pdf-parse to v1 to fix 405 on Vercel

pdf-parse v2 imports @napi-rs/canvas (a native module) at load time,
which crashes the serverless function init on Vercel and causes 405.
v1 has no native dependencies and works in all environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fetchTarFiles 自动检测 gzip 魔数并解压

public.tar 实际为 gzip 压缩（1f 8b 魔数），但 Content-Encoding 头缺失，
CF Worker 的 fetch() 不会自动解压，导致 parseTar 解析原始 gzip 字节失败。

- 检测前两字节是否为 gzip 魔数 (0x1f 0x8b)
- 若是则用 DecompressionStream('gzip') 解压后再传给 parseTar
- 支持 .tar 和 .tar.gz 两种格式

commit.message:
Create SPRINGS music machine - spring mesh physics creates polyrhythms

A physics-based music machine where 30 particles connected by springs bounce and trigger notes at peak displacement. The mesh constantly oscillates (hypnotic before audio starts), and when you press START, each bounce creates a note from D minor pentatonic scale. Tap to perturb particles and watch energy propagate through the spring network.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: revert to filterBy isPrivate-only + joinById for code join

filterBy with composite key ['isPrivate','roomCode'] broke random join —
Colyseus requires ALL indexed fields to match. Reverted to filterBy:['isPrivate']
only. Server generates room code via this.roomId=makeRoomCode() so joinById
can find rooms by code. state.roomCode synced to clients for display.

* feat(m5): PLAYBACK_QOE type validation — require QoE guardrail metrics

Add missing PLAYBACK_QOE validation per design doc §9.3:

Creation: require at least one guardrail config (PLAYBACK_QOE needs
QoE guardrail metrics by definition).

STARTING: validate that at least one metric in the experiment's metric
set (primary, secondary, or guardrail) has is_qoe_metric = true in
metric_definitions. Rolls back to DRAFT on failure.

Adds MetricStore.AnyQoeMetric() for efficient batch QoE check.

4 integration tests: creation without guardrails, start without QoE
metric, valid start, and conclude type details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(m5): fix QoE integration tests — use MEAN metric type and create explicit non-QoE metric

- createQoeMetric: change METRIC_TYPE_RATIO to METRIC_TYPE_MEAN (RATIO requires
  numerator/denominator fields that were missing)
- TestStartExperiment_QoE_RequiresQoeMetric: create explicit non-QoE metric
  instead of relying on seed data for guardrail metric existence
- Add createNonQoeMetric helper for tests needing a metric that exists but
  isn't marked as QoE

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(m5): add chaos test script for management service (milestone 4.5)

Pluggable hook for the chaos_e2e_framework that tests management
service crash recovery:

- chaos_start_management: builds Go binary, starts with Postgres + env
- chaos_health_management: checks /healthz HTTP endpoint
- chaos_verify_management: after crash recovery, verifies:
  - Read path (ListExperiments)
  - Write path (CreateExperiment)
  - State consistency (new experiment is DRAFT, no partial writes)
  - Sentinel experiment survives crash (state persisted in Postgres)
  - Lifecycle transition (StartExperiment still works)

Standalone mode runs a complete kill/recovery cycle with SLA assertion
(<= 2000ms recovery). Integrates with framework via:
  ./scripts/chaos_e2e_framework.sh --services management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(rotation): wall-clock timer, restore auto-resume, hard stop for pinned surfaces

Three interconnected bugs in useRotationCycle:

1. Timer drift — setInterval(e => e + 100, 100) accumulates jitter and
   breaks in background tabs (throttled to 1Hz). Now uses Date.now()
   anchoring: elapsed = accumulated + (now - anchor).

2. Dead auto-resume — tick effect cleanup called clearResumeTimer(),
   killing the 6s timer that pause() just set. Moved resume-timer
   cleanup to a dedicated unmount-only effect.

3. Auto-resume while pinned — pause() always scheduled auto-resume,
   even for explicit user pins. Added stop() (hard pause) and wired
   it to the SurfaceFinder pin effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial build: MediaProof game preservation vault

- Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS dark theme app
- 125+ verified physical game releases (Switch/PS5/PS4/Xbox)
- Offline Tier A/B/C/D certification system
- Personal vault with barcode search, add/remove to collection
- 62 verification lab scenarios with progress ring, streaks, badges
- Public Shareable Certificate pages (summary always visible, evidence locked)
- Stripe checkout/portal/webhook API routes
- Firebase/Firestore integration (therobots-io project)
- Anonymous-first with localStorage, soft sign-in prompt
- Auth flow via therobots.io hub with firebase_token callback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite LegiScan strategy: bulk datasets instead of per-bill calls

Old approach: getMasterList + getBill + getBillText per bill = 3,000+
calls per state, would blow 30k limit in 5 states.

New approach: getDatasetList (1 call) + getDataset per session (~100
calls) = entire country downloaded. Weekly refreshes ~20 calls.

- legiscan-budget.js: soft limit at 1,000/month, emergency stop at
  2,000. Dataset approach should use <200 calls/month total.
- legiscan.js: API client enforces budget check before every call,
  200ms rate limit, logs every call to api_usage table. Returns
  Infinity for monthly count if DB read fails (fail-safe).
- schema.sql: added dataset_cache table to track download hashes
  so unchanged sessions are never re-downloaded.

commit.message:
fix: prune stale flush threads on table removal (#13)

## Summary

- **System fix**: `FlushCoordinator` now prunes stale flush threads,
backpressure counters, and `per_table_lsn` entries when tables are
removed via `remove_table()`. Without this, frozen LSN entries held back
`confirmed_lsn`, preventing slot advancement and causing unbounded lag
growth.
- **Benchmark fix**: `prepare_env()` now queries all existing `sbtest%`
mappings and removes them, plus drops leftover sbtest tables from prior
wider scenarios. Also adds missing `pg_ducklake` extension loading.
- **New regression test**: `remove_table_lag` verifies that removing a
table does not hold back replication for remaining tables.

### Key changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `flush_coordinator.rs` | `prune_removed_tables()` — shuts down stale
threads, reclaims backpressure counters, sweeps `per_table_lsn` |
| `metadata.rs` | `get_all_mapping_ids()` — returns all mapping IDs for
a group |
| `service.rs` | Calls prune after `collect_results()` each cycle
(avoids late-result race) |
| `run_sysbench.py` | Scoped cleanup to `sbtest%`, drops leftover
tables, adds `pg_ducklake` extension |
| `start_db.sh` | Adds `pg_ducklake` to `shared_preload_libraries` |

### Benchmark results (15s OLTP, before → after fix)

| Metric | Single-table | Multi-table | Ratio |
|--------|-------------|-------------|-------|
| Avg lag (append) | 1.7 MB | 2.5 MB | **1.5x** (was 20x) |
| Peak lag (append) | 2.8 MB | 4.6 MB | **1.6x** |
| Avg lag (mixed) | 0.8 MB | 1.4 MB | **1.75x** |

## Test plan

- [x] All 27 regression tests pass (`make installcheck`)
- [x] New `remove_table_lag` test covers the stale-thread scenario
- [x] `bench_suite.sh --duration 15` completes with all 4 scenarios
passing consistency
- [x] Multi-table lag ratio reduced from 20x to ~1.5x (expected for 4
tables sharing one slot)
- [ ] Verify no backpressure leak under sustained remove_table +
add_table churn

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Asset Lesson 08 — Animations (#223)

* Add Asset Lesson 08 — Animations

Extends the glTF parser with animation support. forge_gltf_load() now
parses the "animations" array into structured data (samplers, channels,
keyframes), and a new forge_gltf_anim.h header provides runtime
evaluation with binary search, lerp/slerp interpolation, and automatic
application to node transforms.

Files added:
- common/gltf/forge_gltf_anim.h — runtime animation evaluation
- lessons/assets/08-animations/README.md — lesson walkthrough
- lessons/assets/08-animations/PLAN.md — design decisions and phases

Files modified:
- common/gltf/forge_gltf.h — animation types, parsing, alignment check
- tests/gltf/test_gltf.c — 16 new tests (45 total)
- lessons/assets/README.md — add lesson 08 row
- PLAN.md — mark lessons 07-08 complete, renumber web frontend lessons
- scripts/forge_diagrams/README.md — update stale diagram counts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add pipeline integration for Asset Lesson 08 — Animations

Adds the animation extraction pipeline: a C tool (forge-anim-tool) that
reads glTF animations and writes .fanim binaries, a Python animation
plugin that invokes the tool as a subprocess, and multi-plugin-per-extension
support in the pipeline registry so mesh and animation plugins both handle
.gltf/.glb files independently.

New components:
- tools/anim/main.c — C tool: glTF → .fanim binary + .meta.json sidecar
- tools/anim/CMakeLists.txt — build config with MSVC libm guard
- pipeline/plugins/animation.py — subprocess plugin matching mesh pattern
- tests/pipeline/test_animation.py — 16 tests for the animation plugin

Pipeline changes:
- pipeline/plugin.py — registry maps extensions to list of plugins
- pipeline/__main__.py — per-plugin cache keys, --plugin CLI filter
- tests/pipeline/test_plugin.py — updated for list-based registry

Also updates lesson README and PLAN to cover the full pipeline story.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #223 feedback: skip empty animations, add Key concepts, fix multi-root examples

- Parser: skip animations with zero usable channels instead of exposing
  empty placeholder clips through animation_count
- README: add Key concepts section covering glTF animation structure,
  interpolation, keyframe evaluation, and pipeline integration
- README: fix world transforms examples to loop over all root nodes
  instead of assuming a single root

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #223 round 2: compact samplers, stride alignment, no-op caching

- Parser: compact usable samplers (skip CUBICSPLINE/invalid) and remap
  channel sampler indices so sampler_count reflects only usable entries
- Parser: reject accessors with byteStride not aligned to component size
  (prevents UB on strict-alignment architectures)
- Parser: replace magic 3/4 with FORGE_GLTF_ANIM_VEC3_COMPONENTS and
  FORGE_GLTF_ANIM_QUAT_COMPONENTS named constants
- Pipeline: don't cache plugin results when processed=False (missing tool)
  so installing the tool later triggers reprocessing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Claude/fix lamination edge defaults pgr9 g (#389)

* fix: replace hardcoded pencil_round/arris edge slugs with null defaults

Bug 1 fix (FIX-40MM): quote-setup-defaults.ts and room-presets.ts
hardcoded 'pencil_round' and 'arris' slug strings as default edges.
These slug strings were written directly to the DB, causing phantom
polishing charges (Bug 3) because the calculator treated any non-null
edge value as "finished".

All piece type defaults now return null edges (raw). The tenant-
configurable default_edge_type_id from pricing_settings (QF-1) is
the correct source for edge defaults when set.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: remove thickness>=40 auto-LAMINATED fallback and Mitred→PencilRound enforcement

Bug 2 fix: pricing-calculator-v2.ts forced LAMINATED on any piece with
thickness_mm >= 40, even when lamination_method was NONE. Lamination
method is now purely an explicit user choice.

Bug 6 fix: MiniPieceEditor.tsx used thickness === 40 as isMitred check,
forcing Pencil Round on edge clicks for all 40mm pieces. Now checks
lamination_method === 'MITRED'. Removed pencilRoundId useMemo and the
old Mitred→PencilRound enforcement block entirely — the mitre IS the
finished edge per March 5 correction.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: add lamination method selector for 40mm pieces, fix interface types

Bug 5 fix: Added 3-button selector (None/Laminated/Mitred) shown when
thickness > 20mm, replacing the text-only "Laminated" indicator. Selector
appears in all 3 shape sections (RECTANGLE, L_SHAPE, U_SHAPE).

Added lamination_method to InlinePieceData interface and handleSave
payload. Replaced ugly cast+wrong fallback that inferred LAMINATED
from thickness with proper local state from piece.lamination_method.

Also added lamination_method to MiniPieceEditor WizardPiece interface
to fix TS error from Fix 2.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: update edge type fields to string | null across all interfaces

WizardPieceInput in quote-validation.ts, WizardPiece in
MiniPieceEditor.tsx, and seed-starter-templates.ts all had edges
typed as plain string instead of string | null, causing Railway
build failure at ManualQuoteWizard.tsx:381.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

* fix: FIX-40MM type fix — WizardPieceInput edges nullable

Updated edge fields to string | null in starter-templates.ts and
room-preset-service.ts — the last two interfaces with non-nullable
edge string types. All edge interfaces now consistently use
string | null across the codebase.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01VPoqhVtPs6MR6VeNo5bSNU

---------

commit.message:
fix: タスク管理のリスナーが無音で失敗していた問題を修正

【原因】
startTaskListeners() で where + orderBy の複合クエリを使用していたが
Firestoreの複合インデックスが未作成のためクエリが失敗していた。
onSnapshotにエラーハンドラーがなく完全に無音で落ちていた。

【修正】
- クエリから orderBy('createdAt','desc') を削除し
  受け取ったデータをクライアント側で .sort() するよう変更
  → 単一フィールドのインデックスのみで動作（自動生成済み）
- onSnapshot にエラーハンドラー(err=>) を追加
- submitNewTask 成功後にフォーム入力値・ボタン状態をリセット
- エラー時のアラートにエラーメッセージを表示するよう改善

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Dan avatar initials with human SVG illustration

Dark hair, blue shirt, friendly smile - renders at all sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix share links breaking in messaging apps due to spaces in codes

- URL-encode shareCode with encodeURIComponent() in link generation
  so spaces become %20 and the full URL stays as one clickable link
- Auto-replace spaces with hyphens as admin types share codes
- Sanitize share code on save (replace spaces with hyphens)
- t.html now tries alternate form (spaces↔hyphens) if exact match
  fails, so both old and new links continue to work
